content:
Yes, I am confident that the correct answer is (B) In Ireland, almost everyone speaks English.

While Irish (Gaelic) is an official language of Ireland and is taught in schools, the majority of the population primarily speaks English in daily life. Therefore, saying "they all speak Irish" or similar statements (as in options A, C, D) is inaccurate. Option (E) just says "Irish," which is incomplete as a sentence.

Option (B) accurately reflects the linguistic reality in Ireland.
